reasoning: After an 80%+ gain on GOOGL and sitting at 12% portfolio weight alongside two other ~12% positions (TSM and NXSN), the concentration risk is real. The capex doubling is the most underappreciated story here — it will compress margins and FCF for years. RSI at 25 is a bounce argument, not a buy argument for someone already up 80%. Trim GOOGL by roughly half (sell ~21 shares), bringing it to ~6% of portfolio. Use proceeds to either build cash or add to a position with no geopolitical risk and no capex overhang. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: Reduce ~21 shares now (bring to ~6% of portfolio). Hold remainder with stop-loss at $250 (200d MA failure). No add until FCF trajectory from $175-185B capex becomes clearer — need Q1 2027 earnings to confirm the investment is working.
entryConditions: No add until FCF trajectory from $175-185B capex becomes clearer — need Q1 2027 earnings to confirm the investment is working.
exitConditions: Reduce ~21 shares now (bring to ~6% of portfolio)

Q1 2027 earnings to confirm the investment is working

bullCase: Google Cloud accelerating to 48% YoY is exceptional. RSI at 25 is genuinely oversold — a bounce candidate. $120.8B cash, $74.3B net cash provides a buffer. Analyst consensus at $354 avg target (29% upside). TurboQuant breakthrough validates AI investment thesis. The ad business still generates enormous cash.
bearCase: Doubling capex to $175-185B is not a bull signal — it's an expensive admission that Google is behind in AI and paying to catch up. Revenue growing 18% cannot justify that capex in the near term. FCF will be crushed in 2026. CEO selling $10M is a credibility hit. Three positions (GOOGL, TSM, NXSN) each at ~12% of portfolio — you have 36% of your money in three stocks with overlapping AI/semiconductor exposure and significant geopolitical risk. This is not a portfolio; it's a thematic bet with no diversification.
